explanationZhTw: 表示「大概、推測上」，不是百分之百確定，而是根據線索做判斷。
generatedExample: Presumably, the train will arrive on time.

explanationZhTw: 表示「確認、查明」；這裡指無法把事情完全確定下來。
generatedExample: Please check the details to make certain everything is correct.

explanationZhTw: 表示「沒有窗戶的」，用來描述這個空間完全封閉、沒有自然採光。
generatedExample: They put us in a windowless room for the interview.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的特定用語，指一種兼具螢幕與監視功能的裝置；在這裡屬於設定內詞彙，不是一般日常用法。
generatedExample: The telescreens in the room watched every movement.

explanationZhTw: 「dull aching」指鈍鈍、持續性的痛，不是尖銳劇痛；這裡是在形容腹部隱隱作痛。
generatedExample: After the run, she felt a dull aching in her knees.

explanationZhTw: 「belly」是「肚子、腹部」的口語說法，比較日常。
generatedExample: The child laughed and held his belly.

explanationZhTw: 「bundle A into B」表示匆忙地把某人塞進、推進某處，帶有動作粗魯、沒有多加照顧的感覺。
generatedExample: The guards bundled him into the car and left quickly.

explanationZhTw: 「gnawing」在這裡不是字面上的「啃咬」，而是比喻一種持續折磨人的感覺，像飢餓或焦慮一直在侵蝕你。
generatedExample: She had a gnawing worry that something was wrong.

explanationZhTw: 「unwholesome」在這裡表示「不健康、令人不舒服的」，不是單純指食物不衛生，而是形容這種飢餓感帶有病態、不正常的味道。
generatedExample: The room had an unwholesome smell after being shut for days.

explanationZhTw: 「had not been fed」是被動用法，意思是「一直沒有被給食物／沒有吃到東西」。
generatedExample: The animals had not been fed since yesterday morning.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」在這裡不是一般電視，而是小說中的特殊裝置，兼具螢幕與監視功能，用來監看並警告人。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never seemed to be turned off.

explanationZhTw: 「craving for food」表示對食物的強烈渴望或飢餓感，這裡強調那種越來越難忍受的想吃東西的感覺。
generatedExample: After the hike, his craving for food was so strong that he ate three sandwiches.

explanationZhTw: 「longed for」表示非常渴望、盼望某件事或某樣東西，語氣比一般的「want」更強。
generatedExample: She longed for a quiet place to rest.

explanationZhTw: 「from time to time」是片語，表示「偶爾、時不時地」，說明事情不是一直發生，而是間歇出現。
generatedExample: From time to time, I still check that old folder for forgotten files.

explanationZhTw: 「temptation to find out」指「想要弄清楚的誘惑」，也就是明知有風險，還是很想確認真相。
generatedExample: The temptation to find out what was inside the box was too strong.

explanationZhTw: 「overcame his fear」表示克服了恐懼，最後不再被害怕阻止行動。
generatedExample: Her curiosity overcame her fear, so she opened the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `telescreen` 是《1984》裡的特定裝置，指結合螢幕與監視功能的終端，不是一般日常英文詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen flickered on, and a stern announcement filled the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `cells` 不是「細胞」，而是「牢房」；在監禁或警察語境中是特殊用法。
generatedExample: The guards checked the cells one by one after lights out.

explanationZhTw: 指「暫時拘留所、臨時看守處」，特別是臨時關押被捕者的地方。
generatedExample: After the protest, the police took several people to a temporary lock-up for questioning.

explanationZhTw: 表示「至少、不管怎樣」；這裡是補充一個保守的時間估計。
generatedExample: I am not sure when she left, but it was some time yesterday, at any rate.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「估計、判斷」時間，不是字面上的「量測儀表」。
generatedExample: Without a watch, it was hard to gauge how long the meeting had lasted.

explanationZhTw: 指被人群擠撞、推來推去。
generatedExample: We were jostled on the train during the morning rush hour.

explanationZhTw: 指「舉止、風度、神態」，屬較正式用語。
generatedExample: Her calm demeanour made everyone feel more relaxed.

explanationZhTw: 指財物被查扣、沒收或扣留。
generatedExample: The customs officers impounded the car because it had no valid documents.

explanationZhTw: 指「走私的、偷偷帶進來的」。
generatedExample: They were caught with smuggled cigarettes hidden in a bag.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是指「用大聲喊叫壓過、喝止」。
generatedExample: The audience shouted down the speaker before he could finish.

explanationZhTw: 用來表示「另一方面、相反地」，常用於對比前述內容。
generatedExample: The trip is expensive. On the other hand, it will be a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

explanationZhTw: 表示「關係很好、相處融洽」。
generatedExample: She is on good terms with her former manager.

explanationZhTw: 指「用花言巧語哄騙、軟磨硬泡地要到某物」。
generatedExample: He wheedled an extra day off from his boss.

explanationZhTw: 指門上的「窺視孔、貓眼」，可從外面或裡面偷看。
generatedExample: The guard looked through the spyhole before opening the cell door.

explanationZhTw: 指「寬容、克制、忍讓」，屬較正式的名詞。
generatedExample: The teacher showed forbearance when the class became noisy.

explanationZhTw: 指「強迫勞動營」，即被迫工作的拘禁場所。
generatedExample: Survivors later described the conditions in the forced-labour camps.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「沒問題」，而是指「情況還過得去／能混得下去」。
generatedExample: The job is all right as long as you know what you are doing.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「熟悉門路、懂規矩、知道怎麼做」。
generatedExample: Ask Anna for help; she really knows the ropes here.

explanationZhTw: 指「勒索、非法勾當、敲詐生意」等組織化的非法牟利行為。
generatedExample: The investigation uncovered years of racketeering inside the company.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指「特權階層、上層集團」，不是字面上的貴族。
generatedExample: In that prison, the strongest inmates formed a small aristocracy.

explanationZhTw: 片語，指「來來去去、進進出出的人流或活動」。這裡是在說囚犯不斷被帶進帶出，流動很頻繁。
generatedExample: There was a constant come-and-go of students in the hallway between classes.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「各式各樣的、形形色色的」。這裡強調囚犯類型很多。
generatedExample: The store sells snacks of every description.

explanationZhTw: `combine` 在這裡是「聯手、合力」，`suppress` 是「壓制、制服」。整句表示其他囚犯必須一起合作，才能把那些暴力醉漢制伏。
generatedExample: The teachers combined to suppress the chaos in the classroom.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `wreck` 不是「殘骸」的字面意思，而是指一個人看起來疲憊、失修、狼狽或狀態很差。
generatedExample: After the long hike, he looked like a wreck of a man.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思不是單純「下來」，而是頭髮在掙扎中「散落下來、垂下來」。
generatedExample: Her hair had come down by the end of the concert.

explanationZhTw: `hoist` 在這裡是「費力地拉起、抬起」。整句表示她用力把自己撐直、站穩。
generatedExample: She hoisted herself upright after slipping on the floor.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「滑落、溜下來」。這裡指她從溫斯頓的膝上滑到長凳上。
generatedExample: The book slid off the table and hit the floor.

explanationZhTw: 口語中的禮貌道歉或請求對方原諒，意思接近「不好意思／借過／請原諒」；語氣比一般的 `sorry` 更客氣、也更老派。
generatedExample: Beg pardon, could you repeat the last part of your sentence?

explanationZhTw: 對人的親暱稱呼，常對女性使用；在這裡帶有一點輕慢或過度熟悉的感覺，不一定真是親切。
generatedExample: Come here, dearie, and have a seat.

explanationZhTw: `buggers` 是粗俗口語的罵法，這裡不是字面意思，而是指「那些討厭的人／傢伙」；帶有不滿和髒話色彩。
generatedExample: The buggers next door played music until midnight.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「懂得禮貌地對待女士／女人」；這裡是在抱怨別人不夠尊重她。
generatedExample: He knows how to treat a lady, so he held the door open for her.

explanationZhTw: 方言或口語說法，意思是「我不太像平常的自己／我身體不太舒服」；不是標準文法，但很常見於口語角色台詞。
generatedExample: I'm not feeling right today, so I ain't myself.

explanationZhTw: 「vomited」是「vomit」的過去式，意思是「嘔吐、吐出胃裡的東西」。在這裡是字面用法，描述她真的吐了。
generatedExample: He vomited after the car ride.

explanationZhTw: 「copiously」意思是「大量地、很多地」。這裡用來強調嘔吐的量很多，屬於較書面、較少在日常口語中直接使用的副詞。
generatedExample: It rained copiously throughout the night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「不要把吃下去的東西吐出來／把食物留在胃裡」，不是字面上的「壓低」。
generatedExample: I tried to keep the soup down after I felt sick.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語用法，指「把吃下去的東西吐出來」，不是一般的「舉起來」或「拿起來」。
generatedExample: He got it up a few minutes after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 fresh 不是一般的「新鮮」，而是指「剛吃下去、還在胃裡沒消化」。
generatedExample: Don't lie down right after eating; the food is still fresh on your stomach.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「恢復清醒、醒過來」的意思，不是一般較常見的「復甦、恢復活力」。
generatedExample: After a few seconds, she revived and asked what had happened.

explanationZhTw: 片語「take a fancy to someone」表示「突然喜歡上某人、對某人產生好感」。
generatedExample: My grandmother took a fancy to the new neighbor right away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Wass` 是口語／方言拼法，等於 `What's`，意思是「是什麼／叫什麼」。在這種寫法裡，它帶出人物的口音、年代感或不正式的說話方式。
generatedExample: "Wass the matter with you?" he asked in the same rough dialect.

explanationZhTw: `dearie` 是對人親暱的稱呼，類似「親愛的／小寶貝／小可愛」。在這裡不是字面上的「珍貴」，而是帶有口語、親切，甚至可能有點上了年紀的說話語氣。
generatedExample: "Come here, dearie," the old woman said with a warm smile.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `funny` 不是「好笑」，而是口語中表示「奇怪、出人意料」；`Thass` 是 `That's` 的口語/方言寫法。
generatedExample: That's funny. I thought you left an hour ago.

explanationZhTw: `sentimentally` 表示「帶著感傷、情感化地」；這裡用來表達她說話時有種親切、帶點感情的語氣。
generatedExample: She smiled sentimentally when she saw the old photo.

explanationZhTw: 「physique」指一個人的體格、身材或體型。這裡不是泛指外表，而是強調她的身體條件與年齡是否相符。
generatedExample: Her tall physique made her easy to spot in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「forced-labour camp」在這裡是歷史與政治語境中的「強迫勞動營」，不是一般的工作營。它暗示長期被囚禁、剝削和嚴苛生活造成的身心改變。
generatedExample: He returned from the forced-labour camp looking much older than before.

explanationZhTw: `ordinary` 是「普通的、平常的」，用來對比特別或異常的人事物。
generatedExample: It was just an ordinary day at work.

explanationZhTw: `criminals` 是「罪犯」，指違法的人。
generatedExample: The criminals were caught after a long chase.

explanationZhTw: `ignored` 是「不理會、忽視」的意思，表示這些普通罪犯刻意不去理黨犯。
generatedExample: He ignored the warning and kept walking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `polITS` 是帶貶意的稱呼，指「政治犯／黨犯」之類的人，不是一般標準用法。
generatedExample: They used a slang word for the political prisoners.

explanationZhTw: `terrified` 是「嚇壞了、極度恐懼」的意思。
generatedExample: She was terrified of making a mistake.

explanationZhTw: `above all` 表示「尤其是；最重要的是」，用來點出最重要的部分。
generatedExample: I want a job that is challenging, above all rewarding.

explanationZhTw: `overheard` 是「無意中聽到、偷聽到」的意思。
generatedExample: I overheard their conversation on the train.

explanationZhTw: `din` 是「喧鬧聲、吵雜聲」，通常指很大、很煩人的聲音。
generatedExample: We could barely hear each other over the din of the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `room one-oh-one` 不是單純的房號，而是作品中一個帶有特殊、令人恐懼意味的專有名詞。
generatedExample: Everyone in the story feared what would happen in Room 101.

explanationZhTw: 「took hold of」表示「開始強烈地控制住、侵襲」；這裡是說恐慌突然佔據了他。
generatedExample: A strange silence took hold of the room before anyone spoke.

explanationZhTw: 「galloped」原指馬奔馳；這裡形容心跳像馬奔跑一樣急速。
generatedExample: My heart galloped when I heard the bad news.

explanationZhTw: 「truncheons」指警棍、短棍，用來打人的棍棒。
generatedExample: The officer carried a truncheon on his belt.

explanationZhTw: 「grovelling」原本可指匍匐爬行，這裡帶有卑微求饒、狼狽趴地的意思。
generatedExample: He was grovelling on the ground after the fall.

explanationZhTw: 「fix one's mind on」表示把注意力集中在某人或某事上。
generatedExample: I couldn't fix my mind on the report because of the noise.

explanationZhTw: 「flickering」本義是閃爍不定；用在 hope 上表示希望很微弱、搖擺不定。
generatedExample: She held on to a flickering hope that he would return.

explanationZhTw: 「bite into」這裡不是「咬入」的字面意思，而是指深深切進、刺進肉裡。
generatedExample: The wind seemed to bite into my face on the walk home.

explanationZhTw: 「shrank」在這裡不是單純「縮小」，而是「退縮、畏縮、往後躲」。
generatedExample: She shrank from the loud argument and left the room.

explanationZhTw: 「from moment to moment」表示「一刻接一刻、只活在當下」。
generatedExample: He lived from moment to moment while waiting for the test results.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「計算、估算」的意思。
generatedExample: I calculated the total before I left.

explanationZhTw: 指「數到後來忘了數到哪裡」。
generatedExample: I lost count after the tenth question.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在某個時候；遲早會發生」。
generatedExample: At some point or another, we all need help.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「大白天、光天化日之下」。
generatedExample: The thief was seen in broad daylight.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「漆黑一片、非常黑」。
generatedExample: We walked home in pitch darkness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，指「熄滅（燈光）」。
generatedExample: Please turn out the lights before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 指「含蓄提及、暗指；典故式引用」。
generatedExample: Her speech contained an allusion to Shakespeare.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單指鳥類停棲，而是表示「高高地、懸著似地待在某處」。
generatedExample: The cabin was perched on a steep cliff.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「埋葬」的動作，而是指「深埋在地下、位於很深的地方」。
generatedExample: The bunker was buried deep underground.

explanationZhTw: 「clang」是金屬撞擊時發出的刺耳響聲，這裡用來強調鐵門開啟的沉重感。
generatedExample: The gate opened with a loud clang.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「trim」不是「修剪」，而是形容外表整齊、俐落、線條分明。
generatedExample: She wore a trim jacket and looked ready for work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「閃亮」，而是指因為皮革和制服很光亮，在光線下反射出耀眼效果。
generatedExample: Her silver shoes seemed to glitter all over in the sun.

explanationZhTw: 「motioned」表示用手勢示意，沒有直接說話。
generatedExample: He motioned for me to sit down.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語動詞在這裡是「把某人帶進來」或「押進來」。
generatedExample: The police brought in two witnesses for questioning.

explanationZhTw: 「shambled」指拖著腳、動作笨拙地走，帶有不穩、疲憊或狼狽的感覺。
generatedExample: After the long hike, he shambled into the kitchen.

explanationZhTw: 表示「左右來回地」；這裡用來描寫他動作不安、猶豫。
generatedExample: She swayed from side to side while waiting for the train.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「在牢房裡來回踱步」的意思，不是單純「漫遊」。
generatedExample: He wandered up and down the hallway before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 表示「沒穿鞋、赤腳的」。
generatedExample: After the beach, the child came home shoeless.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「從洞裡伸出來、凸出來」。
generatedExample: Her hair was sticking out from under the hat.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「已經好幾天沒刮鬍子了」。
generatedExample: By Friday, I was several days away from a shave.

explanationZhTw: 指稀疏、雜亂、沒整理好的鬍子。
generatedExample: He returned from the trip with a scrubby beard.

explanationZhTw: air of 在此表示「給人的感覺、氣質」；ruffianism 則是「流氓氣、粗野作風」。
generatedExample: His torn jacket gave him an air of ruffianism.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `roused himself` 是「讓自己振作起來、清醒過來」的意思，語氣帶點勉強，表示他不是自然地變得有精神，而是刻意把自己從遲鈍狀態拉回來。
generatedExample: After lunch, I had to rouse myself and get back to work.

explanationZhTw: `lethargy` 指「無精打采、倦怠、提不起勁」的狀態。
generatedExample: The heat made everyone move with lethargy.

explanationZhTw: `telescreen` 在《1984》裡是非常特殊的詞，指那種兼具播放與監視功能的螢幕裝置；這不是一般日常英文的普通字義。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room seemed to watch every movement.

explanationZhTw: `conceivable` 表示「可以想像的、可能成立的」。
generatedExample: It is conceivable that the plan could fail if the timing is wrong.

explanationZhTw: `bearer` 這裡是「持有者、帶著某物的人」的意思，不是單純字面上的「搬運者」。
generatedExample: She was the bearer of the news everyone had been waiting for.

explanationZhTw: 在《一九八四》裡，`telescreen` 是一種同時播放資訊並監視人的裝置，接近「電幕／監視電視」這種概念。
generatedExample: The telescreen crackled to life before the announcement began.

explanationZhTw: `startled` 意為「受驚的、嚇一跳的」。
generatedExample: She looked startled when the door opened behind her.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「集中注意力」，而是指眼睛慢慢對準、聚焦在溫斯頓身上；這種寫法帶有擬人感，屬於較具畫面感的表達。
generatedExample: After a few seconds, his eyes focused themselves on the map.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `You too!` 不是單純指「你也……」，而是回應對方時的慣用說法，意思接近「你也是」或「彼此彼此」。
generatedExample: ‘I’m glad to see you.’ ‘You too!’

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「你是因為什麼事情被關進來的？」或更一般地說「你是因為什麼原因出現在這裡／陷入這種處境？」在監獄語境中特別常見。
generatedExample: He asked me what I was in for after I mentioned the prison transfer.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「老實說／說真的」。用來先鋪陳真話或較直接的說法。
generatedExample: To tell you the truth, I forgot to do the homework.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「坐下」。這裡描述他完成坐下的動作。
generatedExample: She sat down next to me and started talking.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，表示「笨拙地、不自在地」。這裡帶出人物動作不自然，可能也暗示緊張。
generatedExample: He smiled awkwardly when everyone looked at him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是一般的「冒犯」，而是偏法律或政治語境中的「罪行、違法行為」。在小說裡語氣很正式，也更像審問用語。
generatedExample: In this country, speaking against the law is treated as an offence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `commit` 不是「承諾」，而是指「犯下、做出（某個行為，特別是罪行）」；`committed it` 就是「做了那件事／犯了那件事」。
generatedExample: He admitted that he committed the theft.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Apparently` 不是在強調「明顯地」，而是表示「看來、似乎、據說」；說話者是在轉述一個推測或別人的說法，語氣帶有保留。
generatedExample: Apparently, I left my phone at home.

explanationZhTw: 指「額頭」，也就是眉毛上方到髮際線之間的前額部位。
generatedExample: She wiped sweat from her forehead after the long run.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 temples 不是「寺廟」，而是指「太陽穴」，也就是頭部兩側靠近眼睛上方的位置。
generatedExample: After staring at the screen all day, he pressed his temples to ease the headache.

explanationZhTw: 「indiscretion」指不得體、失禮或欠考慮的舉動；這裡是說他把 "God" 留在行尾，被視為一種失當。
generatedExample: Leaving that detail out was an indiscretion, not a technical necessity.

explanationZhTw: 「definitive edition」是指權威、最終定稿的版本；在出版語境中，表示這是正式且被認可的版本。
generatedExample: The museum released a definitive edition of the artist's catalog.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「line」不是一般的「線」，而是指詩句中的一行文字，屬於文學語境下的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The translator changed the line to fit the rhythm.

explanationZhTw: 「could not help it」是慣用語，表示「無法控制／身不由己」，常用來替自己的行為辯解。
generatedExample: I laughed when I heard the joke; I could not help it.

explanationZhTw: 「rack one's brains」是慣用語，表示絞盡腦汁、費盡心思想辦法。
generatedExample: I racked my brains for a better answer all night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `passed out of` 不是「昏倒」，而是表示「逐漸消失、離開」；意思是那股煩躁從他的表情上退去了。
generatedExample: The tension passed out of her voice as soon as she heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: `warmth` 在這裡不是指溫度，而是指「內在的熱情、愉悅感、腦中的興奮感」；`intellectual warmth` 就是知識帶來的興奮與滿足。
generatedExample: There was an intellectual warmth in her voice when she talked about ancient history.

explanationZhTw: `pedant` 指「賣弄學問的人」或「過度在意細節、規則的人」；這裡帶有一點諷刺意味。
generatedExample: He sounded like a pedant when he corrected everyone's grammar at dinner.

explanationZhTw: `found out` 在這裡是「發現、查出」的意思；指那種學究找到一個無用但讓自己很開心的知識。
generatedExample: I found out that the museum stays open late on Fridays.

explanationZhTw: 這是常見問句片語，意思是「你有沒有想過／你可曾想到」。用來引出一個新觀點或提醒對方注意某件事。
generatedExample: Has it ever occurred to you that we should leave earlier?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `determined by` 不是單純「決定」的動作，而是表示「受到……所左右／由……所造成」。
generatedExample: His choice of words was determined by the audience.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lacks` 表示「缺少、欠缺」，意思是英文沒有押韻形式。
generatedExample: The report lacks clear examples.

explanationZhTw: `rhymes` 在這裡是名詞，指「押韻的字／韻腳」。
generatedExample: This song is easy to remember because it has simple rhymes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `occur to` 不是「發生」，而是「突然想到；進入某人的腦中」。
generatedExample: The idea never occurred to me until she mentioned it.

explanationZhTw: `in the circumstances` 表示「在這種情況下／考量到當時狀況」，是比較正式的說法。
generatedExample: In the circumstances, we decided to postpone the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `strike someone as + 形容詞` 表示「讓某人覺得／看起來像是」，不是「打擊」。
generatedExample: At first, the plan struck me as risky.

explanationZhTw: 指「一天中的什麼時間」，也就是在問現在幾點。這裡是很自然的口語說法。
generatedExample: What time of day is best for meeting the client?

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `arrested` 是「逮捕、拘留」；`arrested me` 表示「把我抓走／逮捕我」。
generatedExample: The police arrested me after the protest.

explanationZhTw: `flitted round` 表示「快速地四處移動、掃視」；這裡形容眼睛很快地在牆上四處看。
generatedExample: Her eyes flitted round the room until she found her friend.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `calculate` 不是單純「計算數字」，而是指「推算、估算過了多久」。
generatedExample: Without a clock, it is hard to calculate the time.

explanationZhTw: 「desultorily」表示散漫地、東一下西一下地，形容談話沒有明確主題、斷斷續續。
generatedExample: The group chatted desultorily until the teacher asked a direct question.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是《1984》裡的特定名詞，指兼具播放與監視功能的螢幕裝置。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the corner never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: 「bade」是動詞 bid 的過去式，這裡是書面或古風用法，意思是命令、吩咐。
generatedExample: The officer bade the soldiers to wait outside.

explanationZhTw: 「fidgeted」表示坐立不安、扭來扭去，常用來形容因緊張或不耐而不停小動作。
generatedExample: He fidgeted during the long interview.

explanationZhTw: 「clasping」表示緊握、抱住，這裡指雙手牢牢抓著膝蓋。
generatedExample: She sat there clasping her bag with both hands.

explanationZhTw: 「lank」形容瘦長而乾枯、缺乏彈性的樣子，常帶點不健康或沒精神的感覺。
generatedExample: His lank hair fell over his eyes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「barked」不是狗叫，而是粗聲粗氣地喝令、吼叫。
generatedExample: The guard barked at us to move faster.

explanationZhTw: 「entrails」是內臟、腸子；在這裡用來表現身體深處突然緊縮的恐懼感。
generatedExample: The sight made his entrails twist with fear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「tramp」是名詞，指沉重連續的腳步聲。
generatedExample: We heard the tramp of boots in the hallway.

explanationZhTw: 形容一個人臉色冷漠、表情不友善；在這裡不是字面上的「臉很冷」，而是指這名軍官看起來冷酷、沒有人情味。
generatedExample: The cold-faced manager answered every question without smiling.

explanationZhTw: 「stepped into」在這裡表示「走進、進入」。這是常見的動作表達，屬於可理解的片語動詞用法。
generatedExample: She stepped into the room and closed the door behind her.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「cell」不是「細胞」，而是指監獄裡的牢房。這是依上下文才能判斷的較特殊意思。
generatedExample: The prisoner was taken back to his cell after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語描述一個很短、很小的手勢；重點在動作輕微而迅速。
generatedExample: She made a brief movement of the hand to stop him talking.

explanationZhTw: 「indicated」在這裡是「示意、指向、表示要某人注意」的意思，不一定只是一般的「指出」。
generatedExample: He indicated the chair with a small nod and waited.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Room 101` 不是一般的「101號房」，而是《一九八四》中的專有名詞，指向最可怕、最能折磨人的地方或情境。
generatedExample: For her, public speaking was Room 101.

explanationZhTw: `clumsily` 指「笨拙地、動作不靈活地」。這裡描寫他的走路姿態，帶出狼狽感。
generatedExample: He walked clumsily across the icy floor.

explanationZhTw: `perturbed` 在這裡是「不安的、受擾亂的」；`vaguely` 表示這種不安只是隱約可見。整個片語屬於較文學、較少見的用法。
generatedExample: She seemed vaguely perturbed by the unexpected question.

explanationZhTw: `uncomprehending` 指「不明白的、茫然不解的」。這裡不是一般口語的說法，而是較正式、書面化的形容詞。
generatedExample: He gave me an uncomprehending look and said nothing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「復活」，而是指疼痛又回來了、重新發作。
generatedExample: The pain in my ankle revived after I started walking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示思緒變得沉重、無力，像往下垮一樣。
generatedExample: After hours of bad news, my confidence sagged.

explanationZhTw: 表示一再重複、繞圈子，沒有進展。
generatedExample: We talked round and round but never reached a decision.

explanationZhTw: 指突然的抽搐或痙攣，常用於身體的短促劇烈收縮。
generatedExample: She felt a spasm in her back after lifting the box.

explanationZhTw: 指內臟，語氣偏文學或較正式。
generatedExample: The explorer described the animal's entrails in the report.

explanationZhTw: 指卡其色，或卡其布料；在這裡是描述衣服顏色。
generatedExample: He packed a pair of khaki pants for the trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是單純「嚇一跳」而已，而是指 Winston 受到驚嚇後，暫時忘了自己的戒備、矜持或平常的自我控制狀態。
generatedExample: The sudden applause startled her into self-forgetfulness, and she smiled openly.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「一瞥、匆匆一看」。這裡不是正式的「看法」，而是很短暫的一眼。
generatedExample: She gave the note a quick glance before putting it away.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「痛苦、悲慘、極度不愉快的狀態」。這裡用來形容他整個人的情緒和處境。
generatedExample: After the accident, he was in misery for weeks.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，指「不平穩地、斷斷續續地、抽動似地」。描述走路動作很不自然。
generatedExample: The bus moved jerkily over the rough road.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，指「明顯地、顯然地」。表示說話者根據情況做出的判斷。
generatedExample: Evidently, no one had checked the door before leaving.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，指「胖乎乎的、略顯臃腫的」。通常帶有外觀描述意味。
generatedExample: The child had pudgy hands and short fingers.

explanationZhTw: 動詞，指「凝視、長時間盯著看」。比一般的看更專注，也更持續。
generatedExample: She spent the afternoon gazing out of the window.

explanationZhTw: 片語，指「中景；不太近也不太遠的距離」。這裡是視線落在前方某個模糊距離。
generatedExample: He stared into the middle distance, lost in thought.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是口語問法，意思是「你是因什麼罪名被關進來的／你為什麼在坐牢？」不是單純的「你正在做什麼」。
generatedExample: He looked at the new prisoner and asked, "What are you in for?"

explanationZhTw: 指《1984》裡的「思想罪」；連想法本身都可能被當成犯罪。
generatedExample: In that regime, even private doubts could be treated as thoughtcrime.

explanationZhTw: 表示「幾乎要哭出來、情緒失控地哭訴」。
generatedExample: He was almost blubbering when he told us what happened.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語稱呼，像「老兄、老弟」；帶點熟稔或討好的語氣。
generatedExample: Cheer up, old chap, it will be fine tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 指「公平聽證／公平申訴機會」，也就是會公正地聽你說明。
generatedExample: Everyone deserves a fair hearing before a decision is made.

explanationZhTw: 口語用法，意思是「聰明的、有腦子的」；這裡是自嘲式的說法。
generatedExample: My sister is brainy and picks up new ideas quickly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「只會被判到……、只會受到較輕的處分」，不是單純「下車」。
generatedExample: If I'm lucky, I might get off with a warning.

explanationZhTw: 指「勞改營、勞動營」；是被強制勞動的拘禁場所。
generatedExample: After the war, he was sent to a labour-camp.

explanationZhTw: 指「行為走偏、失控、脫軌」，常用來說人開始做出不合規矩的事。
generatedExample: He was doing well until he started going off the rails.

explanationZhTw: `guilty` 表示「有罪的；犯了罪的」；在這裡是指 Winston 在詢問對方是否承認有罪或犯錯，屬於法律／道德語境中的常見用法。
generatedExample: The judge asked whether the suspect was guilty.

explanationZhTw: 形容卑屈、諂媚地順從權勢的人或態度。這裡用來寫 Parsons 偷看電幕時那種討好的神情。
generatedExample: He answered with a servile smile and agreed with every order.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的專有名詞，指兼具電視與監視功能的裝置，可譯為「電幕」。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never stopped watching them.

explanationZhTw: 形容假裝道德高尚、裝腔作勢的樣子。這裡指他臉上帶著一種自以為正經的表情。
generatedExample: She gave a sanctimonious speech about honesty after lying all week.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》裡的專有概念，指「思想罪」：連不被允許的想法本身都算犯罪。
generatedExample: In the novel, even doubt can be treated as thoughtcrime.

explanationZhTw: 表示說話方式帶有說教味、格言式、故作正經。這裡是在描述 Parsons 說話很愛下結論。
generatedExample: He spoke sententiously about duty, as if every sentence were a proverb.

explanationZhTw: 形容「陰險而不易察覺」的，常用來說危險、影響或疾病悄悄滲入。
generatedExample: The problem was insidious, so nobody noticed it until it was serious.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡是「逐漸控制、侵入、纏上」的意思，不是字面上的「抓住」。
generatedExample: Fear can get hold of you before you realize it.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「盡自己的一份力、做該做的事」。
generatedExample: I can't fix everything, but I can do my bit.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stuff 不是具體「東西」，而是很口語地指「內容、念頭、雜七雜八的東西」；在這句裡近似「壞念頭」。
generatedExample: I had a lot of stuff on my mind before the interview.

explanationZhTw: 指把聲音壓低、放輕聲說；這裡不是字面上的「下沉」，而是指說話音量變小、變得更低沉。
generatedExample: He sank his voice when he started to share the secret.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「被迫、不得不」；不是一般禮貌上的「感激」，而是有外在原因逼得他必須做某事。
generatedExample: I felt obliged to answer the question honestly.

explanationZhTw: 指「粗鄙語、髒話、淫穢字眼」；這裡是較正式的詞，常用來描述不雅或冒犯性的詞語。
generatedExample: The teacher asked him not to use an obscenity in class.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「一遍又一遍、反覆地」。
generatedExample: He checked the answer over and over again before sending it.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示「只限你我知道；私下說」。
generatedExample: Between you and me, I don't think the plan will work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「得到我」，而是口語中的「逮到我、抓住我、把我抓起來」。
generatedExample: The police got me before I reached the airport.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「出庭、到法庭前受審」的意思，屬於較正式的司法語境。
generatedExample: She had to go up before the tribunal to explain her actions.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`denounced` 不是一般的「譴責」，而是指「向當局告發／檢舉某人」，帶有政治監控或密告的語感。
generatedExample: Someone denounced him to the secret police after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「doleful」指憂鬱的、哀傷的。這裡和「pride」連用，語氣有點矛盾，表示他一方面自豪，一方面又帶點傷感。
generatedExample: She gave a doleful sigh after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 「keyhole」是鑰匙孔；這裡表示她偷聽門內的談話。
generatedExample: He looked through the keyhole to see who was inside.

explanationZhTw: 「nipped off」口語用法，表示很快地跑走、溜走。
generatedExample: She nipped off to the store before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 「patrols」指巡邏隊或巡邏人員。這裡是把事情報給巡邏的人。
generatedExample: The patrols checked the neighborhood every hour.

explanationZhTw: 「nipper」是英式口語，指小孩、毛頭小子。這裡就是「七歲的小孩」的意思。
generatedExample: That little nipper can already ride a bike.

explanationZhTw: 「bear a grudge」是習語，表示對某人懷恨在心、記仇。
generatedExample: I don't bear him any grudge for quitting the team.

explanationZhTw: 「bring up」是片語動詞，意思是養育、扶養小孩長大。
generatedExample: They brought up their children to be honest and kind.

explanationZhTw: 「in the right spirit」表示以正確的態度、用對的方式去做。這裡指他認為女兒的行為符合他所教導的價值觀。
generatedExample: Try to take the feedback in the right spirit.

explanationZhTw: 「jerky movements」指動作不平順、忽快忽慢、很僵硬的動作。這裡是在描述人物身體動作的笨拙與不自然。
generatedExample: After sitting too long, he stood up with jerky movements.

explanationZhTw: 「casting a longing glance」是帶有文學感的說法，意思是「帶著渴望地看了一眼」。這裡強調角色非常想要某樣東西。
generatedExample: She kept casting a longing glance at the bakery window.

explanationZhTw: 「lavatory pan」是較正式或較英式的說法，指馬桶的瓷盆部分，也就是廁所裡的便器。
generatedExample: The cleaner disinfected the lavatory pan before opening time.

explanationZhTw: 「ripped down」在這裡不是字面上把東西撕壞，而是表示「猛然往下拉、迅速扯下」。這是一個帶有動作感的用法。
generatedExample: He ripped down the curtain to let in the sunlight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `old man` 是非正式稱呼，常見於口語，類似「老兄／先生」；不一定真的指「年紀很大的人」。
generatedExample: Old man, could you tell me where the station is?

explanationZhTw: `can't help it` 表示「沒辦法控制／忍不住」，說話者覺得自己無法改變這件事。
generatedExample: I know I should relax, but I can't help it when I hear bad news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `waiting` 是名詞用法，指「等待這件事／等待的過程」，不是單純的動詞現在分詞。
generatedExample: The waiting before the exam was worse than the exam itself.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「plumped」不是一般的「使膨脹」；在這句話中是指「重重地坐下、跌坐進去」，帶有笨重而粗魯的動作感。
generatedExample: He plumped himself down onto the couch after the long day.

explanationZhTw: 「posterior」是比較正式、委婉的說法，指「屁股、臀部」。
generatedExample: The nurse checked his posterior for any signs of irritation.

explanationZhTw: 英式用語，指「馬桶盆／坐便器的盆體」，比日常說法更偏正式或書面。
generatedExample: The plumber replaced the cracked lavatory pan.

explanationZhTw: 「yelled」指大聲喊叫、吼叫；這裡表示聲音很尖銳、強硬地叫出指令。
generatedExample: The coach yelled at the players to line up immediately.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是《1984》中的特殊裝置，兼具電視與監控功能；在這裡指從裝置中傳出的聲音。
generatedExample: In the novel, the telescreen never seems to stop watching people.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「cells」不是一般的「細胞」，而是指監禁用的牢房；在句中是特定語境下的意思。
generatedExample: The prisoners were kept in separate cells overnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `uncovered` 不是「發現」或「揭露秘密」，而是指「把遮住臉的東西移開／露出臉來」。
generatedExample: She uncovered her face after taking off the scarf.

explanationZhTw: `lavatory` 是比較正式的「廁所／洗手間」說法。
generatedExample: The lavatory is at the end of the corridor.

explanationZhTw: `turned out` 是片語動詞，意思是「結果證明是／原來是」。
generatedExample: It turned out that we had the wrong address.

explanationZhTw: `stank abominably` 的意思是「臭得要命／臭得非常厲害」。這裡的 `abominably` 強調氣味很糟。
generatedExample: The rubbish bin stank abominably after two hot days.

explanationZhTw: 此處不是單純「移除」，而是指某人被帶走、撤離原位，帶有被處置的意味。
generatedExample: After the interview, she was removed from the waiting room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示人員持續進出、來來去去，不是字面上只說「來」和「去」。
generatedExample: Visitors came and went all afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 此處指「被送去、被分派到」某個地方，帶有權力強制安排的語氣，不是一般中性的分配。
generatedExample: The old books were consigned to the basement.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是植物乾枯的字面義，而是指因恐懼或壓力而整個人看起來「萎縮、退縮」。
generatedExample: He seemed to shrivel when the manager started shouting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指臉色或膚色因驚恐而改變，重點是「臉色發白／變色」，不是一般的顏色變化。
generatedExample: When he heard the news, he turned a different colour.

explanationZhTw: 表示表面有斑點、色塊不均，常用來形容皮膚、動物毛色或表面紋理。
generatedExample: The bird had mottled feathers that helped it blend in.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是名詞「袋子」，而是形容臉頰下垂、鼓起或像有口袋一樣，帶點動物化的外觀。
generatedExample: His cheeks were pouched from years of smoking.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是把東西藏起來、收在不顯眼的地方。
generatedExample: She had some cash tucked away for emergencies.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示眼神快速、短暫地移動，不是鳥類飛來飛去的字面動作。
generatedExample: Her gaze flitted across the room and stopped at the door.

explanationZhTw: 這個副詞表示膽怯地、畏縮地，帶有害怕被注意到的感覺。
generatedExample: He timorously raised his hand to speak.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是和別人對上眼、與別人目光接觸。
generatedExample: I looked down when I caught the teacher's eye.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「被帶進來、被押進來」的意思；被動語態中的 `bring in` 表示把人帶入某處。
generatedExample: The witness was brought in for a short interview.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「讓人發冷」，而是指他的出現讓溫斯頓瞬間感到害怕或不安。
generatedExample: The unexpected message sent a momentary chill through me.

explanationZhTw: `commonplace` 指平凡的、普通的、沒有特別之處的。
generatedExample: It was a commonplace explanation for a very unusual problem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `mean-looking` 不是「小氣」，而是指看起來兇惡、刻薄、令人不舒服。
generatedExample: A mean-looking guard stood by the door.

explanationZhTw: `emaciation` 指極度消瘦、骨瘦如柴的狀態，通常暗示健康不佳或飢餓。
generatedExample: The doctor was alarmed by the patient's emaciation.

explanationZhTw: `disproportionately` 指「不成比例地、過度地」，這裡是在說嘴巴和眼睛因為臉太瘦而顯得特別大。
generatedExample: The windows looked disproportionately small on the huge wall.

explanationZhTw: 表示「坐下」，是 `sit down` 的過去式。這裡只是描述動作。
generatedExample: She sat down and took a deep breath.

explanationZhTw: 表示「飽受折磨的、痛苦煎熬的」，常用來形容表情、神態或內心狀態。
generatedExample: He had a tormented look after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「像骷髏一樣的」，這裡強調臉部瘦削、可怕、幾乎沒有血色。
generatedExample: The mask had a skull-like face with hollow eyes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「鮮明、清楚到像真的一樣」，常用來形容記憶、畫面或想像。
generatedExample: I still have a vivid memory of that summer.

explanationZhTw: 表示「出了什麼問題／怎麼了」，是很常見的口語說法。
generatedExample: She asked me what was the matter when I started crying.

explanationZhTw: 表示「飢餓致死」或「極度缺乏食物的狀態」，比一般的 `hunger` 嚴重得多。
generatedExample: Many families faced starvation after the harvest failed.

explanationZhTw: 表示「幾乎同時」；`simultaneously` 較正式。
generatedExample: The two alarms went off almost simultaneously.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「攪拌」，而是「些微的動靜、輕微的騷動」。
generatedExample: There was a faint stirring in the audience before the speech began.

explanationZhTw: `drag back` 是「拉回來」；這裡是被動式，表示視線又被吸回去。
generatedExample: My attention kept being dragged back to the loud noise outside.

explanationZhTw: `waddled` 指「搖搖擺擺地走」，`clumsily` 表示笨拙地；合起來強調步伐笨重不穩。
generatedExample: The toddler waddled clumsily across the room.

explanationZhTw: 表示「尷尬而慚愧的、局促不安的」。
generatedExample: He gave an abashed smile after realizing his mistake.

explanationZhTw: 表示「滿是污垢的、髒兮兮的」。
generatedExample: His hands were grimy after fixing the bike.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「生氣的」，而是指「猛烈的、強烈的」；用來形容聲響或動作很激烈。
generatedExample: A furious wind shook the windows all night.

explanationZhTw: 大到幾乎讓人聽不見別的聲音，常用來形容非常吵的聲音。
generatedExample: The audience gave her a deafening cheer.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的虛構裝置，類似電視但也帶有監視功能。
generatedExample: The telescreen stayed on all day in the apartment.

explanationZhTw: 指突然被嚇到或驚到，立刻停住反應。
generatedExample: She jumped in her tracks when she heard the loud bang.

explanationZhTw: 指快速而用力地把手或物體推向某處。
generatedExample: He thrust the letter into my hand and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 「roared」在這裡不是單純指動物吼叫，而是指「大聲吼道、怒喝道」。
generatedExample: The coach roared at the players to stay focused.

explanationZhTw: 「let fall」在這裡表示「讓它掉下來、把它放落」，是個帶有命令語氣的片語。
generatedExample: He let fall the envelope onto the table without saying a word.

explanationZhTw: 「chinless」是形容詞，字面意思是「下巴不明顯的、沒有下巴的」，在這裡用來描述這個男人的外貌特徵。這屬於比較少見的字，用在描述人物時很具體。
generatedExample: The chinless man in the photograph looked stern and silent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是命令式用法，意思是「保持站著、不要離開原地」。
generatedExample: Remain standing until the guard returns.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動詞，意思是「轉向、面向」；不是名詞的「臉」。
generatedExample: Face the window and listen carefully.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般抽象的「運動」，而是指任何身體上的移動；整句就是「不要動」。
generatedExample: He stayed still and made no movement.

explanationZhTw: 意為「服從、照做」，表示他立刻照著命令行動。
generatedExample: The soldier obeyed without asking any questions.

explanationZhTw: 表示「往旁邊讓開、移到一邊」，讓出位置或通道。
generatedExample: She stepped aside so the doctor could pass.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「從某處出現、走出來」；重點是原本被遮住，現在才顯露出來。
generatedExample: A cat emerged from under the sofa.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是「放走」的字面義，而是「使出、揮出」一記重擊。
generatedExample: He let free a hard punch before anyone could stop him.

explanationZhTw: 表示「完全離開、遠離到不碰到」；這裡是被打得幾乎離開地面。
generatedExample: The wave lifted the boat clear of the sand.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「最後撞停、停落在某處」，不是字面上的「去拿來」。
generatedExample: The car skidded and fetched up against the curb.

explanationZhTw: 表示「搖搖晃晃地、不穩地」，帶出受傷或虛弱的狀態。
generatedExample: After the fall, he stood up unsteadily.

explanationZhTw: 「prisoners」指「囚犯、犯人」。這裡是常見詞，屬於基礎到初中階常用字彙，適合理解情境。
generatedExample: The prisoners waited silently for the guard to open the door.

explanationZhTw: 「chinless」是形容詞，字面意思是「沒有下巴的」；在這裡用來強調那個人的外貌特徵，屬於較少見、偏文學性的用法。
generatedExample: The artist drew a chinless figure with a very small face.

explanationZhTw: 「climbed back into his place」這裡不是字面上真的「爬回去」，而是表示他又回到原來的位置或姿勢。屬於帶有動作感的敘述用法。
generatedExample: After speaking, she climbed back into her seat.

explanationZhTw: 「swollen」指「腫起來的、腫脹的」。這裡描述嘴巴因受傷而腫大，是日常常見但在文學描寫中也很重要的字。
generatedExample: Her ankle was swollen after she twisted it on the stairs.

explanationZhTw: 「shapeless」表示「沒有固定形狀的、變形的」。這裡是在強調嘴巴因腫脹而失去原本形狀，屬於描述外觀變化的詞。
generatedExample: The box was crushed into a shapeless lump.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有時候；不時地」，用來說某件事偶爾發生。
generatedExample: From time to time, he still visits the old neighborhood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「滴到……上面」，描述液體慢慢落到某個表面。
generatedExample: Water dripped on to the table from the leaking bottle.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指人體的「胸部」，而是衣服的「前胸位置」。
generatedExample: She pinned the badge to the breast of her jacket.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「快速地移動；一閃一閃地掠過」，常用來形容眼神迅速移動。
generatedExample: Her eyes flitted across the room before she answered.

explanationZhTw: 表示「鄙視；輕視」，是比 `dislike` 更強烈的負面情感。
generatedExample: He felt that the other players despised him after the mistake.

explanationZhTw: 表示「丟臉；羞辱；使人難堪的處境」，屬於較正式的名詞。
generatedExample: She left the meeting in humiliation after being corrected in public.

explanationZhTw: `gesture` 指「手勢、動作」。這裡是很小的手部動作，用來示意別人注意某人。
generatedExample: She made a gesture for me to sit down.

explanationZhTw: `officer` 指「警官；軍官；官員」之類的正式職務稱呼。這裡較可能是指某種執法人員或看守人員。
generatedExample: The officer checked my ID at the gate.

explanationZhTw: `indicated` 在這裡不是單純「表示」，而是「用手勢指示、示意」。屬於根據上下文才看得出的用法。
generatedExample: He indicated the exit with a quick nod.

explanationZhTw: `skull-faced` 是形容臉色或臉型像骷髏一樣，帶有蒼白、瘦削、陰森的感覺。這是偏文學性的非常規用法。
generatedExample: The skull-faced prisoner stared at the wall.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Room 101」不是單純的房號，而是《一九八四》中的特定概念與意象，常指「最可怕、最痛苦的地方」或「個人最深層恐懼的象徵」。
generatedExample: For him, the meeting room felt like Room 101.

explanationZhTw: `gasp` 指「倒抽一口氣、喘息」，這裡表示因驚訝、害怕或緊張而突然吸氣。
generatedExample: She let out a gasp when the door suddenly opened.

explanationZhTw: `a flurry` 在這裡不是字面上的「雪花」，而是指「一陣忙亂、慌張的動作」。
generatedExample: There was a flurry of movement when the announcement started.

explanationZhTw: `flung himself on his knees` 表示「猛地跪下」，強調動作又快又突然。
generatedExample: He flung himself on his knees to beg for mercy.

explanationZhTw: `clasped together` 指「緊緊合攏、扣在一起」，這裡描述手部因緊張或哀求而緊握。
generatedExample: She sat with her hands clasped together in front of her.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是帶政治色彩的稱呼，等於「同志」；不是一般朋友間的稱呼。
generatedExample: Comrade, we need to leave now.

explanationZhTw: 表示「承認、坦白」自己做過的事或知道的事，常帶有被逼供或懺悔的語氣。
generatedExample: He finally confessed that he had lied.

explanationZhTw: 口語慣用語，意思是「立刻、馬上、毫不猶豫地」。
generatedExample: She answered straight off without thinking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的房號，而是《一九八四》裡最可怕的審訊／折磨之地，代表他最恐懼的事情。
generatedExample: He said he would do anything, just not room 101.

explanationZhTw: 在《一九八四》裡，Room 101 不是普通的房間編號，而是帶有特定含義的「101 號房」，通常指極度可怕、令人最恐懼的地方或拷問室。
generatedExample: When she heard the words Room 101, she immediately felt uneasy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `shade` 不是「陰影」，而是指「色調、深淺」。`a shade of green` 就是「偏綠的某種色調」。
generatedExample: Her jacket was a shade of blue that changed in the sunlight.

explanationZhTw: 「yell」是大聲喊叫；這裡表示他情緒激動地吼出來。
generatedExample: He yelled for help from the window.

explanationZhTw: 「finish ... off」是片語動詞，意思是把事情徹底做完；在這裡帶有「乾脆結束我」的強烈語氣。
generatedExample: Please finish it off before dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「sentence」是動詞，意思是「判刑」，不是名詞「句子」；屬於語境中特別的用法。
generatedExample: The judge sentenced him to ten years in prison.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「送出去」，而是「供出、洩漏」某人的資訊。
generatedExample: She refused to give away the password.

explanationZhTw: 口語中表示「我不在乎／我不管」，語氣很強。
generatedExample: I don't care what time we leave.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「袖手旁觀、站著不阻止」。
generatedExample: I couldn't stand by and do nothing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Room 101」不是普通的房號，而是《1984》中一個具有特定象徵意義的地方，通常指用來施加個人最深恐懼的房間，因此屬於情境中的特殊含義。
generatedExample: When he heard the words Room 101, he knew something terrible was about to happen.

explanationZhTw: 「frantically」表示「驚慌失措地、慌亂地」，描寫動作帶有強烈焦急感。這裡不是單純「快速地看」，而是因為恐懼而慌張地四處張望。
generatedExample: She searched frantically for her keys before leaving the house.

explanationZhTw: 「prisoners」是「囚犯」；「other prisoners」指「其他囚犯」。這是基本詞彙，但在小說語境中有助於理解人物所處的受困環境。
generatedExample: The other prisoners stayed silent during the inspection.

explanationZhTw: 「victim」是「受害者、犧牲者」。這裡指他想把另一個人推到自己原本會承受的危險位置。
generatedExample: The victim spoke to the police after the incident.

explanationZhTw: 「settled on」在這裡不是「解決、安定」，而是指眼睛「停留在、定在」某處。這是依上下文決定的動作描寫。
generatedExample: Her gaze settled on the window as the storm began.

explanationZhTw: 「flung out」表示「猛地伸出、甩出」。這裡用來強調動作突然且有力，不只是一般的伸手。
generatedExample: He flung out his hand to stop her from falling.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `bashed` 不是一般的「亂敲」，而是指「狠狠地打、揍」，語氣暴力而粗魯。
generatedExample: He bashed the door with his fist.

explanationZhTw: `Give me a chance` 是慣用說法，意思是「給我一個機會／讓我說明一下」。
generatedExample: Give me a chance to explain what happened.

explanationZhTw: `step forward` 在這裡不是單純「往前走一步」，而是帶有「上前、走出來介入」的動作感。
generatedExample: When the speaker paused, Maria stepped forward to answer.

explanationZhTw: `rise to a shriek` 指聲音「越來越高、最後變成尖叫」，`rose` 這裡是聲音強度和音高上升的意思。
generatedExample: Her voice rose to a shriek when she saw the spider.

explanationZhTw: `telescreen` 是作品中的特定名詞，指一種能傳送影像並監視人的裝置；屬於小說世界裡的專有詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room flashed on without warning.

explanationZhTw: 「flung himself」在這裡不是單純丟出，而是指用很猛、很突然的動作把自己撲出去；帶出強烈的掙扎感。
generatedExample: He flung himself onto the sofa after the match ended.

explanationZhTw: 「grabbed」是快速、用力地抓住，常用來表示一瞬間把東西握住。
generatedExample: She grabbed her bag and ran out the door.

explanationZhTw: 「set up」這裡不是「建立」，而是指引發、造成或開始某種狀態；這裡是他開始發出嚎叫。
generatedExample: The siren set up a wave of panic in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「take hold of」表示抓住、握住某人或某物，通常比單純的 hold 更明確，帶有控制住的意思。
generatedExample: Take hold of the handle before you open the door.

explanationZhTw: 「wrench ... loose」是用強力硬生生扯開、拉開，不是一般的輕拉；這裡強調暴力與吃力。
generatedExample: It took two people to wrench the stuck drawer loose.

explanationZhTw: 「clung on」表示緊緊抓住不放，常用來寫人在壓力或危險中死命不鬆手。
generatedExample: She clung on to the rope until help arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「hauling」不是單純搬運，而是用力拖拉、硬拉，語氣很費力。
generatedExample: They were hauling the canoe up the riverbank.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Room 101` 不是一般的「101號房間」而已，而是《一九八四》中的特定名詞，指極端恐懼與折磨的地方；屬於情境上非常特殊的用法。
generatedExample: Everyone in the story feared Room 101.

explanationZhTw: `officer` 指「警官、軍官、官員」之類的職稱或公職人員；這是常見基礎詞彙。
generatedExample: The officer opened the door and looked inside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `led out` 是指「被帶出去、押出去」，不是單純的「帶領出去」；在這個情境中帶有被制服後帶離現場的意思。
generatedExample: The guard led out the prisoner after the hearing.

explanationZhTw: `nursing` 在這裡不是「當護士」，而是指「小心地護著、顧著」受傷的手，帶出受傷後的疼痛與保護動作。
generatedExample: She walked home, nursing her sprained ankle.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「他已經完全失去鬥志／反抗力」，表示人整個被擊垮了。
generatedExample: After hours of arguing, all the fight had gone out of him.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 unreproved 指「沒有被責備、沒有被制止」；語氣偏文學，比一般的「not criticized」更正式。
generatedExample: He kept talking, unreproved by the teacher.

explanationZhTw: gave way to 表示「被…取代；轉而變成」。這裡是飢餓感慢慢變成口渴感。
generatedExample: My excitement gave way to worry when the train was late.

explanationZhTw: unvarying 指「不變的、持續一樣的」；這裡強調聲音和燈光一直沒有變化。
generatedExample: The room had an unvarying level of noise all night.

explanationZhTw: induced 在這裡是「引起、造成」，不是比較專門的醫學用法。
generatedExample: The heat induced a feeling of tiredness.

explanationZhTw: bearable 表示「可忍受的、能承受的」；no longer bearable 就是「再也無法忍受」。
generatedExample: The pain was just bearable after he took the medicine.

explanationZhTw: stay on his feet 是慣用說法，意思是「站得住、沒有跌倒」；這裡指他太暈而無法保持站立。
generatedExample: After the long hike, I could barely stay on my feet.

explanationZhTw: concealed 表示「隱藏的、藏起來的」；這裡是刀片被偷偷藏在食物裡。
generatedExample: The letter was concealed inside the book.

explanationZhTw: suffering 這裡是「受苦、忍受痛苦」，可指身體或精神上的痛苦。
generatedExample: He was suffering after the accident.

explanationZhTw: merely 表示「只是、僅僅」；語氣常用來縮小前面內容的份量。
generatedExample: It was merely a small mistake.

explanationZhTw: foreknowledge 指「事先知道」；這裡更具體是「預先知道自己將要受痛苦」。
generatedExample: She acted with foreknowledge of the risks.

explanationZhTw: answerable 在這裡不是單純的「可回答的」，而是「目前還無法得到答案的」。
generatedExample: The problem is not answerable at the moment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `boots` 不是單指靴子本身，而是借代「穿著靴子的人」或「軍靴聲所代表的人正在接近」；在小說語境中帶有緊張、壓迫感。
generatedExample: The boots outside the door made everyone nervous.

explanationZhTw: `came in` 是片語動詞，表示「進來、進入」；這裡是走進房間的意思。
generatedExample: She came in quietly and closed the door behind her.

explanationZhTw: 表示他突然站起身來，帶有迅速、受驚後立刻反應的語氣。
generatedExample: She started to her feet when she heard the alarm.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 shock 指「強烈的震驚／驚嚇」，不是字面上的電擊。
generatedExample: The shock of the news made him sit down.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的專有名詞，指同時具有螢幕與監視功能的裝置。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room reminded everyone that they were being watched.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `got you` 不是單純「拿到你」的字面意思，而是指「他們也逮到你了／抓到你了」。在這種語境中，`get` 有「抓住、逮捕、控制住」的含義，屬於情境性用法。
generatedExample: The police have got you now, so stop running.

explanationZhTw: 「step aside」表示往旁邊讓開、退到一邊，這裡是指他讓出位置。
generatedExample: Please step aside so the nurse can get through.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「emerged」不是抽象的「浮現」，而是指從後方走出、現身。
generatedExample: A small dog emerged from under the table.

explanationZhTw: 「truncheon」是警棍、短棍，通常指執法人員或警衛攜帶的打擊工具。
generatedExample: The security guard carried a truncheon at his belt.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `deceive yourself` 不是指一般的「欺騙別人」，而是指「自欺欺人、對自己說謊、明知如此卻不肯承認」。在這段對話裡，O'Brien 是在逼 Winston 面對他其實早就知道的事實。
generatedExample: Stop deceiving yourself; you know this plan will not work.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「眼中只有、只專注於」；這裡指他完全盯著那根警棍，別的都不在意。
generatedExample: She had eyes for the map and ignored everything else.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「警棍、短棍」；這裡是守衛手上拿來施暴的棍子。
generatedExample: The officer held a truncheon at his side.

explanationZhTw: 此處不是「王冠」，而是「頭頂」；屬於身體部位的特殊語境用法。
generatedExample: He rubbed the crown of his head after the bump.

explanationZhTw: 名詞片語，指「上臂」；這裡列出的是可能被打到的身體部位。
generatedExample: The nurse checked the bruise on her upper arm.

explanationZhTw: 指身體無力地垮下來，這裡是因劇痛而整個人倒下。
generatedExample: He slumped onto the sofa after the long hike.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是醫學上的癱瘓，而是形容痛得幾乎動不了。
generatedExample: The cold left her fingers paralysed for a few minutes.

explanationZhTw: 此處表示「受傷的、被重創的」，不是單純的「打擊」。
generatedExample: The company was stricken by the sudden loss of its founder.

explanationZhTw: 不是物理上的爆炸，而是形容光線猛然迸開、突然充滿視野。
generatedExample: The crowd exploded into applause when the winner appeared.

explanationZhTw: 指痛苦扭曲的動作或表情。
generatedExample: The baby made little contortions while stretching.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無論如何／至少」，用來承接前文並收束結論。
generatedExample: At any rate, we should leave before dark.

explanationZhTw: 表示「面對、在...情況下」，強調即使有痛苦也不改變判斷。
generatedExample: In the face of criticism, she stayed calm.

explanationZhTw: 指因劇痛而在地上扭動、翻滾。
generatedExample: He writhed in pain after the injury.
